item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I adore this. Like TheMightyMcB said in their comment, I scrolled up to give it a +1 before I even finished the log. Unlike them, I didn't rescind that vote, but I do agree that the article would be strengthened by reigning in the technical details at the end (just don't lose the Windows 98 bit).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The entity then exits the alleyway via a series of increasingly high triple backflips.

You had me at the cartoonish back-flipping exit.

Seriously though, the idea is interesting and the dialogue is brilliant. Equal parts wacky and pretty goddamn scary. I agree with some of the other comments that the technical information after the log is kind of bland (honestly I found myself losing focus and just scanning over it a bit actually) but this is still a strong +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m a sexy horrible bastard and I’m here to loosen the glands of American audiences everywhere.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Others have commented on other things I like about this already but can we get some appreciation for the fact that it's a straight robot made of plastic and conductive muscle gel? No cloned living tissue, no carbon fibre muscles, no metal skeleton, no magic or thaumaturgy or hume field fuckery, no "it's physiologically identical to a human and we can't explain its superhuman strength" or other series 1-esque handwave, the actual composition of the thing is genuinely unusual compared to your average human-impersonating killer robot. +1 for that alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The entity really had lots of name for the victim and he is OP.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great misdirection here. At frist i fell for it just being a bland android-murderer, until it got… interesting.

I really like the absurdity here, how it turns together with the gore into grotesque humor. Just my style. Especially advanced semi-biological quantum computing matrices in conjunction with a slightly modified version of the outdated Windows 98 got me laughing.

But eventually, i can't upvote this, because it is yet another anomalous company. When it comes to "organisations manufacturing/selling anomalous tech/androids" we already got MC&D, Anderson Robotics and Prometheus Labs, when it comes to "incompetent alien forces selling stuff to humans" we have Dado, and then there is also Stuff Inc., thought they are less alien. And i am defenitly missing on some here.

What i want to say: this concept is so overused, it turned shallow and off-putting. It suffices to deliver the concept, but fails to be truly unique. A small blemish on an otherwise great SCP.

I am staying at a no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ll take twelve!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I was so ready to upvote this by the end of that murder log that I scrolled up and did so, only to scroll back down and find that more description was waiting for me to dash my childish glee to the rocks. It was bad enough that I found myself begrudgingly rescinding my upvote. Really feels like the article shot itself in the foot with that inclusion and I would gladly change my vote if literally every word after the log was just deleted outright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"(SCP-4866 removes its sunglasses, revealing a second, smaller pair of sunglasses underneath the first. It snaps the sunglasses in half, throws the pieces over its shoulders, then points at Burns.)"

holy FUCKING shit, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know that you provided an image of one instance, but I can't help but imagine Jon Favreau blowing a man's skull to bits before backflipping like a madman.

+1 for that imagery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4866 removes its sunglasses, revealing a second, smaller pair of sunglasses underneath the first.

I laughed, yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Instead of a Little Mister, it's a Big Murderer (tee em).

I wonder what other models Optimark will offer. For some reason, the name makes me think it's a subsidiary of a resort timeshare outfit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is the first article in some time that's made me just go "what the fuck?" and for that reason alone it's an easy upvote. Heartily seconding Tanhony's comments as well. If there are more Optimark things in the works I'm interested to see where it goes.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Criticism
item.content:encoded:
This was really enjoyable.

+ Tanhony rambles
- TURN IT OFF

Just a few things that jumped out at me:

SCP-4866 removes its sunglasses, revealing a second, smaller pair of sunglasses underneath the first.

The surreal humour is great here, and it also works as an indicator of just how weird this anomaly is.

Hey there, pal. You look like the kind of guy that understands quality when he sees it. Am I right? You’ve got the ligaments of a discerning organism. A cool cat out and about looking for trouble on the down-low. That's fly. In fact, I've got something that'll impress the shit out of every one of your nociceptors so bad that they won't know what debrided them.

Your dialogue is always fantastic, Cadaver, and this is no exception. The mixture of an inhuman perspective and the very human salesman attitude comes across really well here.

(SCP-4866 turns to look directly into the security camera’s lens.)

Everything after this is just incredible. You’ve built up the tension with the intimidation from the anomaly throughout the log before this, and the shift here breaks that tension with some humour, but not in a way that undercuts the build-up before this.

I feel like I have to agree a little with what others have said about the description of the anomaly’s make-up after the transcription not feeling fully necessary. It’s not something that bothered me too much, but it does feel like the article ramps down in intensity after the high of the transcript.

Initial investigation has revealed that a corporation operating with the name “Optimark Industrial, Inc.” exists in several financial registries

I heard about this Optimark thing that’s getting going, and I have to say that this is a great first impression for it. I’m definitely interested in seeing more of what they’re about!

+1

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to Tanhony or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to agree with ARD here. The Windows 98 footnote was the only worthwhile line in the three "anatomical description" paragraphs. You could cut them all and the article wouldn't suffer at all, I don't think — it's good because it's surreal, not because it's realistic. "Synthetic humanoid construct" leaves enough to the imagination to fill in the gaps; throw a "non-biological" in there somewhere and you've basically conveyed exactly the same narrative information in a twentieth of the space.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted because I enjoyed the humorous contrast between the Billy Mays-esque dialogue and the cartoonish ultraviolence, but I concur with Vezaz and Pedantique regarding the weaknesses in the text surrounding that dialogue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my. I think I'm gonna love this new GoI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the above feedback is probably trending too close to vehemence, I agree that the article would be improved by tightening up some of the longer running sentences and wordier descriptions. I personally value concise language over precise language though, so take that with a grain of salt.

On a different note, the pacing of this article felt off to me. The transcript seems like its strongest element, but after that concludes it goes back to describing comparatively unexciting anatomical features. Though I don't think it could end on the transcript, I do think the amount of text between the end of that section and the end of the article slows the pace down an unfortunate amount.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So absurd it could only be CadaverCommander.+1, cheers

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read this prior to posting and I love it then and I love it now :D

A great introduction to OPTIMARK Industries. I'm legitimately excited to see more extremely optimal products from these industry experts.

The humor in this is top notch while still being fairly unsettling. The idea of this huge, sweaty, bombastic dude approaching me in an alleyway before taking a chomp out a knife is just… well it sure is something.

I'd love to see some tales involving this guy for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it was all quite excellent and well written. To each their own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-4866 squeezes Burns’s cranium with its hand, rupturing it. Burns ceases all movement and vocalization."

Cranium, rupture, "ceases vocalization" - Half the lines in this scp sound like they were beaten to death with a thesaurus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
because the surrounding text is so overwrought and inconsistent.

If it was possible for me to disagree more, I think I might create a disagreement singularity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article abounds with subject verb tense agreement problems, wildly inconsistent tone, and grievous abuse of pseudo-scientific language.

It swings wildly between overly complex, in an attempt to sound technical, and absurdly informal.

"The shrapnel leaves SCP-4866’s mouth at a speed great enough to produce a report not unlike that of a firearm and strike sparks from the asphalt.)"

"A report not unlike that of a firearm" instead of merely "a loud bang" or something similar is ridiculous.

I can't concentrate on the humor of the big fat guy who crushes people to death with his kool-aid man belly and cartoon logic because the surrounding text is so overwrought and inconsistent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so goddamn well written and amazing. It's fun to read, it's exciting, and jesus CHRIST do I need 87 tales with this guy. Cadaver is so good at characterization, it's hard to believe. Here's to 9 small payments of $999.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this article, due to the anomaly's quirky, yet gory ways of killing people, and its questionable methods of advertising.

+1

item.title: buy now! buy now! buy now!
item.content:encoded:
big savings! very optimal! get your 𝘔𝘶𝘳𝘥𝘦𝘳𝘦𝘳™ today!

big thanks to Woedenaz for looking over this and performing his graphics wizardry.

have an optimal day, reader ♥
